items.title: Markets Sell Off -0.5% to -1.8% Ahead of Inflation Rate Thursday
items.description: This marks the fourth-straight down-day for the blue-chip Dow and the comprehensive S&P 500.

items.title: Heard on the Street Recap: Another AI Selloff
items.description: The Dow fell 228 points led by Caterpillar and Nvidia.  The PHLX Semiconductor Index that is home to 30 major chip stocks dropped 3.8% by the closing bell.  Nvidia, Broadcom, Tesla and Google-parent Alphabet all shed 3-4% by the closing bell, racking up a total of $431 billion in lost market cap for the day.

items.title: AI-Themed Stocks Take More Hits, Sending Nasdaq Lower
items.description: Tech stocks slid on Wednesday, continuing to drag down major indexes, as investors worried about the future of AI-themed stocks.  The Nasdaq Composite Index dropped 1.8%, while the S&P 500 slipped 1.2%, the biggest falls for each benchmark in nearly a month.  Tech giants Google-parent  Alphabet Nvidia  and  Tesla  all fell more than 3%, leading the Magnificent Seven growth stocks lower, while the Dow industrials fell 0.5%, dragged lower by a fall in  Caterpillar  shares.

items.title: Tech Slide Weighs on Nasdaq; Oil Prices Gain
items.description: A fresh fall in tech stocks dragged indexes lower Wednesday as concerns about artificial-intelligence valuations and spending heated up.

items.title: Jim Cramer Recommends Deere Over Cummins (CMI)
items.description: Cummins Inc. (NYSE:CMI) is one of the stocks that Jim Cramer shared his take on. A club member mentioned that they have been considering CMI, CAT, and DE and were leaning more toward CMI. Cramer remarked: “Cummins got a lot of data center exposure because of their engines. Caterpillar’s just been straight up. How about Deere? Deere […]
